data.title: U.S. rig count again ticks lower in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of oil and gas rigs actively drilling in the U.S. fell by 1 to 543 during the past week, according to the latest survey reported Friday by Baker Hughes.

During the week ended January 16, drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. rose by 1 to 410 while gas rigs dropped 2 to 122, and 11 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down by 37, or 6.4%, from the same time a year ago, while rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. were down 68 from a year ago and rigs directed at natural gas came in 24 ahead of the same week last year.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin stayed flat at 244, the Eagle Ford court added 1 to 29, and Williston Basin remain unchanged at 28.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Eastman Chemical cut at RBC as weak demand hurts volume, earnings
data.description:
Eastman Chemical (EMN) -2.5% in Friday's trading as RBC Capital downgraded to Sector Perform from Outperform with a $70 price target, saying the stock appears fairly valued and citing continued weak demand for durables and sustainable products, and limited organic growth opportunities.

Given ongoing consumer weakness, RBC analyst Arun Viswanathan said he does not expect a material upside surprise on methanolysis earnings, fibers continued to see a downward long-term trend, and new project capital investment likely will be pushed out by underperformance of the recent Kingsport ramp-up.

Viswanathan said Eastman's (EMN) advanced materials segment - which creates high-performance polymers, films and plastics - has been suffering from weak durables demand and asset utilization headwinds, and continues to see sluggish uptake of methanolysis products given weak consumer sentiment, reducing his 2026 estimate by 3% to $364M.

The analyst said the additives and functional products unit - a key division that manufactures specialty chemicals and additives for diverse markets - has been somewhat steadier, although he expects only a ~4% step-up in 2026 EBIT to $532M, while spreads in the chemical intermediates segment remain challenged, with 2026 likely to remain weak.

data.title: Bayer gains after U.S. Supreme Court grants hearing on Roundup suits
data.description:
Bayer (BAYZF) ADRs gained 5% after the U.S. Supreme Court agreed to hear the chemical conglomerate's appeal that targets thousands of lawsuits that claim its Roundup weedkiller causes cancer. 

The Supreme Court agreed to hear Bayer's appeal of a $1.25 million Missouri jury verdict over Roundup on claims that the 2023 case was preempted by federal law, according to media reports on Friday, including Bloomberg.

Roundup litigation has been a major issue for Bayer (BAYZF) since it purchased Monsanto for $66 billion in 2018. The German conglomerate has paid more than $10 billion in verdicts and settlements related to Roundup and is still facing about 67,000 claims from plaintiffs. 

Bayer (BAYZF) won support from the Trump administration for its appeal last month when U.S. Solicitor General D. John Sauer recommended that the Supreme Court  hear Bayer’s challenge to the Missouri jury verdict. 

Bayer has indicated it is exploring further legal and regulatory actions, including possible restrictions or discontinuation of glyphosate-based product sales, to mitigate financial exposure from ongoing litigation.

data.title: Societe Generale weighs relocating Manhattan headquarters - report
data.description:
French lender Societe Generale (SCGLF) (SCGLY) is scouting roughly 500K square feet of Manhattan office space as it weighs relocating its New York headquarters from the 245 Park Ave. skyscraper, according to a Friday media report.

Vornado Realty's (VNO) 15 Penn Plaza could be a potential new location, as well as 175 Park Ave. and Hudson Boulevard, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar. The bank's lease at 245 Park matures in 2032.

A number of large financial firms have been active in prime Manhattan real estate as of late. JPMorgan Chase (JPM) in October opened its new global headquarters in midtown, and Moody's (MCO) has said it will move its global base to to 200 Liberty Street at Brookfield Place in Lower Manhattan from the 7 World Trade Center building.

Bloomberg said that spokespeople for Societe Generale (SCGLF) and SL Green Realty (SLG), the owner of 245 Park Ave., declined to comment.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Intel earnings, drama in Davos, and the fight for Warner Bros.
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - January 19

  • All day - The U.S. stock markets will be closed for observance of the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday.
  • Spotlight on Davos - The World Economic Forum will hold its flagship annual meeting in Davos, bringing together political, business, and civil society leaders. Program priorities include responsible deployment of AI and other advanced technologies, unlocking new growth, and rebuilding trust between nations and within societies. Notable executives on the program include Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang, Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella, Salesforce (CRM) CEO Marc Benioff, PepsiCo (PEP) CEO Ramon Laguarta, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon, and Goldman Sachs (GS) CEO David Solomon. Politicians due to appear include President Trump, Canada Prime Minister Mark Carney, France President Emmanuel Macron, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, and UN Secretary-General António Guterres.
  • All day - The ICE Barcelona gaming event will begin. The gambling and gaming industry trade show is one of the most notable of the year. Light & Wonder (LNWO), Aristocrat Gaming, and Brightstar Lottery (BRSL) are some of the notable casino industry suppliers that will show off new products.

 

Tuesday - January 20

  • Volatility watch - Firefly Aerospace (FLY) and AXT (AXTI) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include High Roller Technologies (ROLR), Zion Oil (ZNOG), and Springview (SPHL). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include GoDaddy (GDDY), ZipRecruiter (ZIP), and Domo (DOMO). Traders will be watching Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for more drama with the hostile tender offer expiring. Paramount could extend the date.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Netflix (NFLX), 3M Company (MMM), U.S. Bancorp (USB), United Airlines (UAL), KeyCorp (KEY), and Interactive Brokers (IBKR).
  • IPO watch - Crypto player BitGo (BTGO), Japanese-themed restaurant operator Riku Dining Group (RIKU), and consumer lifestyle products provider Aigo Holding (AIGO) are expected to price their IPOs and begin trading during the week. The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of McGraw Hill (MH) and Diginex (DGNX).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Caterpillar (CAT), Dell Technologies (DELL), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), and Pfizer (PFE).
  • All day - Walmart (WMT) will be officially added to the Nasdaq 100 at the beginning of the trading day.
  • All day - Aytu BioPharma (AYTU) will hold an Investor Day event in New York City.

 

Wednesday - January 21

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Charles Schwab (SCHW), Kinder Morgan (KMI), Travelers Companies (TRV), Ally Financial (ALLY), and Halliburton (HAL).
  • All day - The Supreme Court will hear oral arguments on President Trump's efforts to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook
  • All day - Simulations Plus (SLP) will hold a virtual Investor Day event.
  • All day - The Automotive World Tokyo event will begin. The large-scale exhibition and conference will be focused on advanced automotive technologies. Speakers will include representatives from Toyota Motor (TM), Nissan (NSANY), Honda (HMC), Mazda (MZDAY), and Suzuki (SZKMY).
  • All day - The IEA will publish the monthly oil market report.

 

Thursday - January 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include GE Aerospace (GE), Procter & Gamble (PG), Intel (INTC), Abbott Laboratories (ABT), Capital One (COF), and CSX (CSX). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for Mobileye Global (MBLY) after it reports.
  • M&A spotlight - The go-shop period expires on the private equity bid for Clearwater Analytics Holdings (CWAN). Shareholders with Sotherly Hotels (SOHO) vote on the buyout offer. Shareholders with Forge Global Holdings (FRGE) vote on the acquisition offer from Charles Schwab (SCHW).
  • All day - The Bureau of Economic Analysis will issue its latest update on Q3 GDP.
  • All day - The core PCE price index report for October and November will be released. Economists forecast a 2.8% mark for November.

 

Friday - January 23

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include SLB Limited (SLB) and Booz Allen (BAH). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for Alaska Air Group (ALK) after it reports.
  • 10:00 a.m. The IEA will publish its Q1 gas market report.
  • 10:00 a.m. The release of the preliminary University of Michigan survey for January will be closely watched by economists for the update on consumer sentiment and inflation expectations.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story

data.title: Philip Morris International shares climb seven straight sessions
data.description:
Philip Morris International (PM) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 0.7% at $173.86 on Friday.

The tobacco giant gained 8.7% in the preceding six sessions. The stock surged over 32% in the year 2025, compared to a 16.7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

PM is up 10% over the past one month.

Most recently, Philip Morris highlighted that the company is strongly committed to investing in America, with over $20B of investments made since 2022, the majority of which were noted to be made to acquire and further invest in U.S. manufacturing capabilities, commercial rights and infrastructure, and U.S. jobs.

"Philip Morris International (PM) remains attractive due to robust dividend growth and strong positioning in the smoke-free segment," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, PM has a Buy rating with a score of 3.9 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D+ in valuation.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 14 analysts gave PM a Buy and above rating. Four analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while none recommended Sell.

 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: Mid to low cap healthcare stocks with the strongest earnings momentum
data.description:
As the earnings season unfolds, mid to low market capitalization healthcare stocks with strong earnings momentum are drawing investor attention.

The EPS Revision Grade measures the trend in analyst earnings estimates for a company, reflecting how Wall Street’s expectations are shifting over time.

The list below focuses specifically on stocks that have received the highest possible grade, A+, indicating recent positive momentum in analyst projections.

The following healthcare stocks currently hold an A+ EPS Revision Grade:

  1. Aldeyra Therapeutics (ALDX) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  2. Altimmune (ALT) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  3. Annovis Bio (ANVS) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  4. Assertio Holdings (ASRT) — EPS Revision Grade: A+ 
  5. Actinium Pharmaceuticals (ATNM) — EPS Revision Grade: A+
  6. Avanos Medical (AVNS) — EPS Revision Grade: A+

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH)

data.title: U.S. stocks seesaw, even as some chipmakers advance
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday searched for direction, with losses in utility stocks offset by gains in some chipmakers. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) struggled for momentum.

The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was last +0.06% in afternoon trading, while the S&P (SP500) rose +0.12%. The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) edged up +0.07%.  

Chipmakers extended their gains from the previous session in which they were buoyed by strong results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and bargain hunting. Micron (MU) +5.87%, Lam Research (LRCX) +2.45%, and AMD (AMD) +1.74% were among the top percentage gainers on the Nasdaq (COMP:IND).

Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 5.5 basis points to 4.23%, and the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.61%. The longer-dated U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) increased 4.3 basis points to 4.84%.

Top S&P 500 gainers included Super Micro Computer (SMCI) +9.62%, Micron (MU) +5.87%, and GE Vernova (GEV) +5.47%. Decliners included Constellation Energy (CEG) -11.52%, Vistra (VST) -8.26%, and Amcor (AMCR) -7.19%.

data.title: Dominion wins court OK to resume Virginia offshore wind project stopped by Trump
data.description:
A U.S. District Court judge ruled Friday that Dominion Energy (D) can resume work on its Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project while the company continues its legal challenge of the Trump administration's order to stop the $11.2B development.

The order issued by the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia granted Dominion's (D) request for a preliminary injunction blocking the federal government from enforcing its stop-work order after the company said it was suffering irreparable harm and losing millions of dollars every day the project sits idle.

The ruling was the third legal defeat this week to President Trump's effort to stop offshore wind development, and the Virginia judge echoed concerns expressed in the other cases, saying the government had failed to show that any national security risk should halt the Dominion (D) project and that the government's order likely was arbitrary and capricious.

Dominion (D) already has spent nearly $9B on the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, which is expected to provide enough energy to power 600K homes.

data.title: Honeywell shares uptick for seven consecutive sessions
data.description:
Honeywell International (HON) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 2% at $219.37 on Friday.

The industrial conglomerate gained nearly 5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock slipped over 13% in the year 2025, compared to a 16.7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

HON is up 11% over the past one month.

Most recently, Honeywell (HON) confirmed that it intends to file for an IPO of its quantum unit Quantinuum, which was last valued at $10B, with offering details yet to be set.

"Quantinuum’s spinoff will clean up HON’s financials by removing a loss-making segment, supporting clearer reporting and strategic capital redeployment," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Earlier in the day, J.P. Morgan upgraded shares and raised the price target on HON, saying the stock has extended its discount to a sum-of-the-parts valuation, comfortably off its highs compared to SOTP peers that are up a weighted 20%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, HON has a Hold rating with a score of 2.9 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D in growth.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 12 analysts gave HON a Buy and above rating. 13 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Sell.

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.
